name: Pydantic
what_it_does: Python data validation library that uses type hints to define schemas and automatically validates, parses, and serializes data — the foundation of FastAPI's request/response validation and a standard for defining structured agent tool inputs and outputs.
use_cases: Defining strongly-typed input/output schemas for agent tools so agents can validate data before processing
Parsing and validating LLM-generated structured output (JSON) against a schema before use in agent pipelines
Serializing Python objects to JSON with type-safe field definitions for agent-to-agent data interchange
Generating JSON Schema from Python models so agents can introspect expected data structures dynamically
Building configuration models with environment variable parsing (pydantic-settings) for agent runtime configuration
not_for: Runtime schema validation of arbitrary untrusted data at web-scale throughput — consider marshmallow or cerberus for different trade-offs
Languages other than Python — Pydantic is Python-specific; use Zod (TypeScript) or Joi (JavaScript) for JS ecosystems
Simple scripts where Python's built-in dataclasses or TypedDict are sufficient without validation overhead
best_when: You are building Python agent tools or FastAPI endpoints where you need automatic validation of structured inputs, JSON Schema generation, and type-safe data models with minimal boilerplate.
avoid_when: You are in a non-Python stack or need validation logic that goes beyond type coercion and constraint checking — complex cross-field business rules are better handled in separate validation layers.

auth.notes: Library — no auth required to install or use. Pydantic is a local validation library with no network calls or authentication concepts.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free and open source — MIT licensed, no usage limits
pricing.notes: Pydantic core library is MIT-licensed open source. Pydantic Logfire (observability service by the same team) is a separate commercial product; the validation library itself is completely free.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Pydantic v2 produces structured ValidationError with a list of errors, each containing loc (field path as tuple), msg (human-readable), type (error code), and input (the bad value). This format is one of the best for agent consumption — agents can iterate errors and auto-correct LLM-generated JSON that fails validation.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Model validation is deterministic and side-effect-free — same input always produces same output or same error. Agents can safely retry validation without concern for idempotency.
known_agent_gotchas: Pydantic v2 (2.0+) has significant breaking API changes from v1 — @validator is replaced by @field_validator, orm_mode becomes model_config with from_attributes=True; agent code targeting one version will fail on the other
model_validate() coerces types by default (string '123' to int 123) — agents must use model_validate(data, strict=True) if type coercion is not desired
model.dict() is deprecated in v2 in favor of model.model_dump() — agent code using the old method will get deprecation warnings that eventually become errors
Discriminated unions require a Literal type discriminator field — agents generating polymorphic schemas must include the discriminator field or validation silently matches the wrong variant
JSON Schema generated by model.model_json_schema() may differ between Pydantic versions — agents caching schemas must invalidate on library upgrades

security_score_components.security_notes: Pydantic v2 core is implemented in Rust (pydantic-core) which eliminates entire classes of memory safety vulnerabilities present in pure-Python validation. No pickle deserialization. Secret fields (SecretStr, SecretBytes) prevent accidental logging of sensitive values. Validators run arbitrary Python so custom validators with user input could be vectors if not carefully reviewed. pydantic-settings handles environment variable secrets safely.
